concerns.text: Task T032 implements Egger's test but lacks the conditional logic mandated by FR-005 to check if N ≥ 10 before execution. The spec requires the system to flag the test as 'underpowered' and skip it if N < 10. Implementing the test unconditionally violates the conditional constraint.

Task T033 implements trim-and-fill but omits the explicit requirement from FR-005 to 'report the adjusted effect size' in the final output. The task description focuses on the algorithm execution but does not mandate the inclusion of the adjusted metric in the report, creating a semantic gap in the deliverable.

Task T030 implements the random-effects model but fails to include the conditional logic required by FR-004 and the plan to select the estimator based on study count (REML if N<20, DL if N≥20). The task description implies a static implementation, violating the dynamic selection constraint.

Task T012 implements the search query builder and CSV fallback but lacks a specific step to validate that manual CSVs contain the mandatory metadata fields (DOI, publication year, etc.) defined in FR-001. Without this validation task, the system risks silent failure on malformed manual inputs, violating the data integrity constraint.

Task T022 implements the Hedges g calculation but does not explicitly include the requirement from US-2 (Acceptance Scenario 1) to document the formula reference ('Hedges & Olkin, 1985') in the output fields. This omission risks failing the 'Verified Accuracy' requirement for citation tracing in the final report.

The task list lacks any implementation task for 'Moderator Analysis'. The spec's Edge Cases section mandates that if I² > 75%, a moderator analysis MUST be automatically triggered. The absence of a task to build or execute this engine means a critical conditional requirement in the spec is unimplemented.
